title: Chapter 1 Master Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of Java basics, structure, and printing.

questions.question: Java code is translated into which format by the compiler?
questions.explanation: Java code is compiled into Bytecode, which the JVM understands.

questions.question: Java's philosophy is 'Write Once, Run Anywhere'.
questions.explanation: The JVM enables this platform-independent nature.

questions.question: What runs the Bytecode in Java?
questions.explanation: The JVM (Java Virtual Machine) executes the bytecode.

questions.question: What is the topmost container for all your Java code?
questions.explanation: Every line of Java code that runs must be inside a Class.

questions.question: What is the correct way to output 'Hello' and move to the next line?
questions.explanation: println adds a new line automatically after printing.

questions.question: What does a single-line comment start with?
questions.explanation: In Java, // begins a single-line comment.

questions.question: Which of these starts a multi-line comment?
questions.explanation: Multi-line comments start with /* and end with */.

questions.question: What is the exact name of the method where Java execution begins?
questions.explanation: Java programs always start execution from the main() method.

questions.question: Comments are executed by the compiler to improve performance.
questions.explanation: Comments are completely ignored by the compiler. They are meant for humans.

questions.question: What happens if you use System.out.print() repeatedly?
options: It prints everything on the same line.
It prints each on a new line.
questions.explanation: print() does not move to a new line, so subsequent prints are output side-by-side.

questions.question: Which punctuation mark typically ends a Java statement?
questions.explanation: In Java, a semicolon marks the end of a statement.

questions.question: What encloses the body of a Class or Method?
questions.explanation: Curly braces {} are used to define blocks of code in Java.

questions.question: Java is an Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) language.
questions.explanation: Java is deeply based on OOP principles, where everything revolves around Objects.

questions.question: Where do Statements live?
questions.explanation: Statements make up the body of a Method.

questions.question: How do you close a multi-line comment?
questions.explanation: You close a multi-line comment with */.

questions.question: Look at this code: System.out.println(Hello); Why is it invalid?
options: It is missing quotes around Hello.
questions.explanation: String literals must be enclosed in double quotes.

questions.question: Why is the JVM important?
options: It is the only way to write code.
It writes the code for you.
questions.explanation: The JVM allows Bytecode to run on any machine, making Java platform-independent.

questions.question: System.out.println() can be written outside of a Class and Method.
questions.explanation: It MUST be inside a Method, which MUST be inside a Class.

questions.question: What is wrong with this code: class main {}
options: Class names should start with a capital letter by convention.
questions.explanation: By convention, Class names should start with an uppercase letter, e.g., 'class Main'.

questions.question: Complete the phrase: "Write Once..."
questions.explanation: The promise of Java is 'Write Once, Run Anywhere' thanks to the JVM.

questions.question: Every line of code that can execute must be placed inside a _____
questions.explanation: A Class is the basic structural unit in Java.

questions.question: Use ____ to print text to the screen and drop to a new line.
questions.explanation: System.out.println is the correct method.
